function.name: _edit_table_field
function.description:
Edit a single data table field. Uses single-field editing mode, allowing complete and exclusive parameter structures for different field types, suitable for field-level fine-tuning and complex association definitions. Field types and related configurations must strictly follow the definition rules. Must use real table IDs and field IDs. Must call _query_table_list_designer and _query_table_define_designer to get the real structure before calling this tool;

[Unified Expression Usage Rules]
For parameters in this tool that are [Informat Expression Fields] (including but not limited to: editableExpression, visibleExpression, requiredExpression, defaultValueExpression, defaultValueRuleList[].expression, iDSetting.expression, and all fields ending with *Expression), AI must first call the system tool `_read_informat_expression_doc` to read the Informat expression specification before filling them in.

If this tool is not called, no expression content may be generated; the call must be supplemented first.

[Expression Syntax Strong Constraints]. If roles need to be used in expressions, first call _query_app_define_designer in advance to get the role list in the application.

[Expression Context Description]
All fields ending with *Expression in this interface (including but not limited to visibleExpression, editableExpression, defaultValueExpression, defaultValueRuleList.expression, etc.) run in a unified expression context and support the following built-in variables and functions: - user: current logged-in user object
  - user.roleList: user role ID list
  - user.departmentList: user department ID list
- Date / Math / Array / String / Misc: system built-in utility functions

Expressions need to be wrapped in ${ }, supporting multi-line expressions, ternary operators (?:), logical judgments, function calls, and complex calculations.

field.description: Single field configuration (only one field allowed per operation).

method.description:
Operation method. Only one of the following values can be used:
  add    = add field (name, type, and icon must also be provided)
  edit   = edit field (updateFieldList must also be provided to declare the property paths to be modified)
  delete = delete field

updateFieldList.description: List of field property paths explicitly to be modified in this editing operation. Supports field-level and object internal property-level updates. Only effective when method=edit, used to precisely declare the fields involved in this update to avoid incorrect updates caused by null or default fields.

id.description: Field ID (camelCase, must exist; new field ID when add).

newId.description: New field ID, only allowed when method=edit.

type.description:
Field type. [Strict constraint] Only the values listed below can be used. Using any type not listed is strictly prohibited and will be considered a serious error.

[Allowed field type values (must match the spelling exactly)]:
SingleText (single-line text), MultiText (multi-line text), RichText (rich text), Integer (integer), Double (decimal), Date (date), ListSelect (dropdown select), Attachment (attachment), Formula (database computed field), Checkbox (checkbox), User (user), Department (department), Coordinate (coordinate), Relation (relation list), RelationRollup (relation rollup), RelationRecord (foreign key field), RelationRecordField (foreign key field mapping), Children (tree structure), ID (business ID), LookupList (lookup list), LookupRollup (lookup rollup)

Type descriptions:
- ID: Used for business identifiers (such as order numbers or asset numbers). The value is automatically generated by the system and is not a technical primary key.
- RelationRecord: A foreign key field that references a record in another table (stores the target record ID).
- RelationRecordField: A foreign key field mapping. It reads and displays a field value from the referenced table through RelationRecord (data is not stored).
  Usage rule: RelationRecord must be defined before defining the dependent RelationRecordField.
- Children: [Structural field] Used only to build tree/hierarchical structures within the same table and cannot span across tables.
- LookupList: A lookup list used to model the child record collection in a 1:N relationship. The association condition must be explicitly defined using filter.
- LookupRollup: Performs aggregation statistics on child table records based on the RelationRecord foreign key field, and the result is displayed in the main table.
- Relation: A relation list field used to represent weak associations or many-to-many relationships.
- RelationRollup: Performs aggregation statistics on related records based on the Relation field in the current table. It is only applicable to one-to-many Relation with a clear direction.

icon.description:
Field icon identifier. Only the following values can be used (must match the spelling exactly; do not invent any other icons):
text, number-1, checkbox-circle, attachment, check, user, map-2, link, node-tree, hashtag

displayWidth.description:
Display width of the field in the table. Only one of the following integer values can be used: 25, 50, 75, 100. The default value is 50.
For Children, LookupList, Relation, and RichText type fields, displayWidth must be set to 100; for other types, 50 is recommended.

readonly.description: Can configure the field to be non-editable in the form, default false, meaning all can be edited

editableExpression.description: Configure expression for field editability in the form, expression can reference current form record (formRecord) / current user user and other context objects and expression functions. Only effective when readonly is set to true. If the expression execution result is true, the field can be edited. For example: ${Array.containsAny(user.roleList,['admin'])}, meaning only administrators can edit

hidden.description: Can configure the field to be hidden in the form, default false, meaning not hidden

visibleExpression.description: Configure expression for field visibility in the form, expression can reference current form record (formRecord) / current user user and other context objects and expression functions. Only effective when hidden is set to true. If the expression execution result is true, the field is visible. For example: ${Array.containsAny(user.roleList,['admin'])}, meaning only administrators can see this field

requiredExpression.description: Field required condition expression. When the expression returns true, the field must be filled in the form. The expression runs in a unified context and can reference formRecord / user / expression functions.

defaultValueExpression.description: Field default value expression, only effective when creating new records or when the field is empty. Example 1: ${Math.ceil(Math.random()*10000)}; Example 2: ${Date.dateAdd(Date.sysdate(),'day_of_year',1)}; Example 3: ${Array.of('o1','o3')}

defaultValueRuleList.description: Field linkage calculation rule list. When the value of the specified field changes, automatically recalculate the value of the current field.

fieldId.description: Field ID to monitor for changes. When the value of this field changes, it will trigger recalculation of the current field.

expression.description: Expression used to calculate the new value of the current field. Supports multi-line expressions wrapped in ${ }, ternary operators (?:), logical judgments, and built-in functions. The expression can reference current form record (record) / current user user and other context objects and expression functions, for example: ${record.price * record.count}
examples: ${ record.startDate == null || record.endDate == null ? 0 : Date.dateDiff(record.endDate, record.startDate) }

nullable.description: Whether it can be empty. Default true

multiple.description: Whether multiple selection is allowed. Default false

attachmentSetting.description: Required when type is Attachment, used to define attachments.

acceptTypes.description: Only accept the following file type suffixes, such as .png, .jpg, etc. Empty by default means no file type restrictions

formulaSetting.description:
Required when type is Formula. Formula is a [Database Calculation Field], whose calculation logic is fully executed by PostgreSQL, not Informat expressions or front-end expressions.

Important notes (please strictly follow):
- formula is not an Informat expression, does not support ${ }, if, ternary operators, or any Informat built-in functions
- formula must be a valid PostgreSQL expression (SELECT clause level)
- only PostgreSQL built-in functions, operators, and SQL syntax are allowed
- for conditional judgments, please use SQL's case when syntax instead of if
[Field Reference Strong Constraint Rules]:
- formula only allows referencing [fields actually stored in the database in this table] as calculation elements
- strictly prohibited from referencing the following types of fields:
  1) Associated record fields (RelationRecord / RelationList, etc.)
  2) Non-stored calculation fields (such as Formula, Expression type fields)
  3) Fields from other data tables (cross-table references are prohibited)
- field names appearing in formula must have corresponding physical storage columns in the current data table

Applicable scenarios:
- String concatenation, numerical calculation, conditional derivation
- Amount totaling, status calculation, field derivation display

The value of this field is dynamically calculated by the database during query, usually a read-only field.

formula.description:
PostgreSQL calculation expression (not Informat expression).

Rule description:
- The expression must comply with PostgreSQL 13 syntax specifications
- Can directly use this table's field IDs as column names for calculation
- Supports PostgreSQL built-in functions and SQL expressions
- Does not support ${ }, if, ?: and other Informat expression syntax

Examples:
- concat(name, '-', grade)
- price * quantity
- coalesce(actual_cost, 0) - coalesce(budget, 0)
- case when progress >= 100 then 'Completed' else 'In Progress' end

checkboxSetting.description: Required when type is Checkbox, used to define checkboxes.

defaultValue.description: Whether checked, default value is false

relationSetting.description:
Association list (Relation), used to model many-to-many (M:N) relationships between two data tables. The system maintains the mapping between both parties through intermediate association relationships.

Applicable scenarios:
- A record can be associated with multiple target records
- A target record can also be associated with multiple current records

Typical examples:
- Student ↔ Honor (a student can obtain multiple honors, an honor can belong to multiple students)
- User ↔ Role (a user has multiple roles, a role has multiple users)

Inapplicable scenarios:
- If the target record can only belong to one of the current records, please use LookupList.

Note:
The Relation field itself does not represent foreign key ownership, but rather a peer-to-peer association relationship.

relationRollupSetting.description:
Required when type is RelationRollup. RelationRollup summary field definition:

Based on the Relation (association list) field in the current table, aggregates and counts associated records and displays the result in the current table.

Note:
- Only applicable to Relations with clear directionality (recommended for one-to-many relationships)
- Rollup is not recommended for many-to-many Relations. The Relation field pointed to by relationFieldId already contains the target table definition (relationSetting.tableId), and the target table for RelationRollup is implicitly determined by the Relation field, no additional configuration is needed.

relationFieldId.description: Relation type field ID in the current table (association list field).

func.description: Aggregation calculation method. Only one of the following values can be used: count (count), avg (average), sum (sum), max (maximum), min (minimum).

rollupFieldId.description: Field ID in the child table for aggregation calculation (not required for count, required for others).

nullable.description: Whether it can be empty. Default true

lookupListSetting.description:
Lookup list (LookupList), used to model child table record lists in one-to-many (1:N) relationships.

Important notes:
- LookupList does not automatically establish parent-child table associations by default.
- Must explicitly specify association conditions between child table records and the current main table record through filter.
- If filter is not configured, all records of the target table will be returned, which is usually an incorrect usage in one-to-many relationship modeling.

Typical usage:
Use child table foreign key field equals current main table record ID (${record.id}) through filter.conditionList to only return child table data belonging to the current record.

filter.description: Filter conditions used to limit the range of child table records. Usually used to establish associations between child table foreign keys and the current main table record. The system will not automatically infer foreign key fields, must be explicitly configured.

fieldId.description: Usually the foreign key field ID in the child table

opt.description: Comparison operator, optional values: eq, ne, gt, ge, lt, le, contains, notcontains, startswith, endswith, isnull, isnotnull, in, notin, between, notbetween, parenteq, parentrooteq, parentcontains, multipleandquery, multipleorquery, intree. Undefined operators must not be used.

var.description: Whether the comparison value is an expression, default true

value.description: Comparison value, usually using ${record.id} to reference the current main table record

lookupRollupSetting.description:
Required when type is LookupRollup. LookupRollup summary field definition:

Used to aggregate and count one-to-many (1:N) child table records and display the result in the main table.

Working method:
- Based on the RelationRecord foreign key field in the child table, associate to the current main table record
- Perform aggregation calculations on child table records that meet the conditions

Note:
- count does not require rollupFieldId
- sum / avg / max / min must specify rollupFieldId

relationRecordFieldId.description: RelationRecord foreign key field ID in the child table that points to the current main table (note: not RelationRecordField).

func.description: Aggregation method. Only one of the following values can be used: count (count), avg (average), sum (sum), max (maximum), min (minimum).

rollupFieldId.description: Summary field of the child table. Required when the summary method is sum, max, min, or average.

childrenSetting.description: Required when type is Children. Only used to build parent-child hierarchy (tree structure) within the same table, mainly used for organizational structure / category tree and other scenarios.

nullable.description: Whether it can be empty. Default true

iDSetting.description: Required when type is ID, used to define numbering fields.

expression.description: Numbering calculation expression, can use seq as an auto-increment sequence variable, seq is a database auto-increment sequence. If you want the numbering to be date-based with 6-digit increment, such as 2024-07-19-000001, you can use the following expression: ${Misc.formatDate(Date.sysdate(),'yyyy-MM-dd')}-${String.lpad(seq,4,'0')}

resetType.description:
Method for automatically resetting the seq sequence. Only one of the following values can be used:
day (reset at 00:00 every day), month (reset at 00:00 on the 1st day of each month), year (reset at 00:00 on January 1st every year).
